title: Steampunk Corset Forge
description: Players operate a steampunk workshop where they must attach spinning gear mechanisms onto a corset mannequin to power it up. Each gear placement affects connected gears through a conservation-of-torque system, and players must reach a target total torque by strategically placing, rotating, and swapping gears across multiple mounting points while using undo/redo to correct mistakes.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader from CDN. Load Corset.glb centered on a pedestal and gears.glb off to the side as a parts tray. Scene: dark steampunk workshop with warm point lights (orange/amber), subtle fog, and a metallic environment. Camera uses OrbitControls locked to a 120-degree arc around the corset.

Gameplay: Define 6 mounting points on the corset (chest, waist-left, waist-right, back-upper, back-lower, hip) shown as glowing circular indicators. Clone the gears model to create a tray of 5 differently-scaled gear instances (scales 0.5x to 1.5x) on a wooden shelf to the right. Display a HUD showing current total torque, target torque (value 100), and step counter.

Interactions (10-14 steps required): Players click a gear from the tray to select it (highlight with emissive glow), then click a mounting point to attach it. Gears snap to the mount position and begin spinning. Implement a conservation-of-torque law: each gear contributes torque = size * speed, and placing a gear on an adjacent mount reduces neighboring gear speeds proportionally so total system energy is conserved. Players can click an attached gear to detach it back to the tray, or drag between two mounted gears to swap their positions. Provide Undo (Z key) and Redo (Y key) supporting full state history of all placements, removals, and swaps. Gears visually spin using animation loop with speed proportional to their current torque contribution. When total torque equals the target within a tolerance of ±3, all gears glow gold and a victory particle burst triggers. Add a reset button. Show a tooltip on hover over each mount point displaying its coupling relationships. All gear animations should use smooth tweening for attach/detach transitions.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  totalTorque: number,         // sum of torque from all attached gears (torque = size * speed)
  targetTorque: number,         // goal torque value player must reach (fixed at 100)
  stepCounter: number,         // count of gear attach/detach/swap actions performed
  attachedGearCount: number,         // how many gears are currently mounted on corset
  selectedGearIndex: number,         // index of gear currently selected from tray (-1 if none)
  isGearSelected: boolean,         // true when player has picked a gear from tray
  averageGearSpeed: number,         // mean rotation speed of all attached gears in rad/s
  largestGearSize: number,         // scale factor of the biggest gear currently attached
  torqueDeficit: number,         // difference between targetTorque and totalTorque (can be negative)
  hasReachedTarget: boolean,         // true when totalTorque >= targetTorque
  consecutiveSwaps: number,         // count of gear swap operations without detaching first
}
